mnemonics: Cats Purr Nicely On Soft Trees: Constituency, Parsing, Nodes, Objects, Sentences, Trees - to remember the core concepts of analyzing sentence structure.
keyPoints: Constituency describes how words group together to form phrases and sentences.
A constituent is a string of words that acts as a single unit within a hierarchical structure.
Constituency is formally represented through phrase structure trees and bracketing.
commonMistakes: Assuming the linear order of words defines a constituent, rather than the hierarchical grouping.
Confusing constituents (groups based on structure) with dependencies (relationships between individual words).
